description: Locale-aware display capability for the United States market. When fully realized, enables correct date formatting (MM/DD/YYYY), currency display (USD), number formatting, address formatting (US postal conventions), and measurement unit defaults for US-market tenants.

readiness.evidenceSummary: Locale pack manifest (locale-us.json) declares en-US locale with date format (MM/DD/YYYY), currency (USD), and number formatting conventions. Configuration keys are declared. No implemented locale configuration or runtime artifacts exist yet.
readiness.scopeBounds: en-US locale only. Date, currency (USD), and number formatting identified but not implemented.
constrainingFactors: locale-us pack is draft — no runtime locale configuration exists.
No locale-switching infrastructure is implemented in the platform.
No unit testing of US-specific formatting rules.

packDependencies.rationale: US locale-aware display is directly provided by the US locale pack.

claimPosture.permittedScopeStatement: en-US locale conventions have been identified in a planning-stage pack manifest. No locale configuration, formatting logic, or runtime artifacts exist.
prohibitedClaims: Must not claim 'US locale support' or 'localized for US market'.
Must not claim date, currency, or number formatting is implemented.

visibilityPosture.rationale: US locale support is visible in control-plane for planning but blocked until locale pack reaches published state and runtime configuration exists.

activationConstraints: locale-us pack must reach at least published lifecycle state.
Locale-switching infrastructure must be implemented in the platform.

notes: en-US is the closest locale to the platform's current default English baseline, making it relatively low-effort to implement compared to non-English locales.
VistA itself uses US date conventions internally (FileMan dates), so en-US locale alignment with VistA data is naturally high.
